perturbation.original_text: 5. RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" to market the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'sell and distribute' with 'market' for "Exclusive Distribution License Rights". This introduces a contradiction. The term "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" initially includes the right to 'sell and distribute'. However, by limiting it to just 'market', it is uncertain whether CLIENT can actually 'sell' the products exclusively or just market them. The Reserved Rights section then states that CLIENT has un-exclusive rights to sell and distribute the product, which further contradicts if the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" grant exclusive selling rights or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. EXCLUSIVE RIGHTS: Bosch hereby grants to Client the exclusive rights to sell and distribute the Product, subject to the Territory as set forth below, to certain select companies in the Automotive Industry, each of which shall be approved by Bosch in writing as requested by the Client on a case by case basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted the client the 'Exclusive Distribution License Rights' to 'sell and distribute' the products. The changed text limits these rights to only 'market' the products, while still granting un-exclusive rights to 'sell and distribute' in the same territory. This directly contradicts Section 6, which grants 'exclusive rights to sell and distribute'. By changing 'sell and distribute' to 'market' in Section 5, the 'exclusive rights to sell and distribute' granted in Section 6 is contradicted because the client no longer has exclusive selling rights per the modified Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will exercise and authorize any other Party to to take action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.
perturbation.explanation: The negation 'not' is removed from the sentence: 'CLIENT will *not* exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.'. This completely changes the intention of the agreement as previously CLIENT was not allowed to impinge on the other party's rights. This contradicts the purpose of granting exclusive and reserved rights in the first place and creates an obligation for CLIENT to undermine those rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" to market the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the negation 'not', directly contradicting the original intent. The original text stated the CLIENT 'will not exercise nor authorize...any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.' The changed text now states the opposite, that the CLIENT 'will exercise and authorize any other Party to to take action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.' This creates a direct conflict with section 5, 'RIGHTS GRANTED', which grants BOSCH exclusive and reserved rights. The changed text compels the CLIENT to undermine the rights granted to BOSCH, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: Notwithstanding its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH may assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall assist' to 'may assist' changes BOSCH's obligation to assist CLIENT into a discretionary action. This creates a contradiction because earlier sections may imply BOSCH has a duty to support CLIENT's distribution efforts. Now, BOSCH has the option, but no explicit obligation, to provide support. If it does not assist, then there is an impact on what was agreed, and since the client has to fulfill the cost of the product, as per section 8 (iv), the change contradicts this because it impacts the client on the fulfillment of those obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that BOSCH 'shall assist' CLIENT, creating an obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' making assistance discretionary. The contradicted text (iv) states the client is responsible for all expenses related to sales and marketing. If BOSCH now has the option not to assist, it potentially undermines the client's ability to fulfill their responsibilities as per (iv), because BOSCH's assistance was mandatory under the original agreement. This creates a strong, direct conflict and logical inconsistency regarding BOSCH's obligations and the client's responsibilities, triggering a contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
